spec_match.rationale: Page exercises the concept interactively but stops short of touching the real API surface — the underlying spec behaviour is simulated rather than invoked.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive controls drive the page (1 buttons, 1 inputs, 1 selects, 0 ranges, 3 listeners) — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Some unsupported / error handling is wired up, but several spec edge cases (boundary inputs, alternate modes, error states) are not surfaced.

references.rationale: Spec is linked but the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link required by CLAUDE.md rule 3 is missing.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

openQuestions.title: Exercise more of the Spec-compliant JSON MIME type detection spec surface
openQuestions.detail: The spec (https://mimesniff.spec.whatwg.org/#json-mime-type) covers more than the page demonstrates. Walk back through the spec's exposed methods, properties, or modes and add controls for the ones that materially change behaviour. Today the page only hints at a subset.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate failure and edge-case branches
openQuestions.detail: Add visible branches for the unsupported / unavailable / error / boundary inputs paths. For built-in AI APIs, that means 'downloadable', 'downloading', 'unavailable' availability states; for HTTP features, error responses and CSP blocks; for CSS features, zero / extreme / inherited values. Today only the happy path is reachable from the UI.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature link
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule 3 requires every page to embed https://chromestatus.com/feature/5470594816278528. The self-heal slug-fix script reads this link to recover from misslugged folders. Add it under a 'see also' section.

openQuestions.title: Mirror the spec's stated motivation in the demo
openQuestions.detail: ChromeStatus motivation reads: 'Without this change, developers must rely on inconsistent MIME sniffing behavior across browsers, which can lead to incorrect content handling. Aligning with the spec ensures predictable behavior and improves interoperability....'. The demo should make that motivation visible — show the problem the spec is solving (before/after, with-without, attacker view) so a reader leaves understanding why the feature ships, not just what it does.

summary: '+json MIME Explorer' delivers the core idea but leaves spec edges, blurb framing, or design-system polish on the table. The open questions list captures the rough edges worth closing in the next pass.
